title: Birational geometry of complex hyperbolic manifolds
speaker: Gabriele di Cerbo
abstract: In 1984 Hirzebruch constructed the first examples of smooth toroidal compactifications of ball quotients with non-nef canonical divisor. In this talk, I will show that if the dimension is greater or equal than three then such examples cannot exist. We will use this result to reprove and improve classical theorems, such as boundedness of hyperbolic manifolds, Baily-Borel embeddings and cusps count.

proof_stages.label: Toroidal compactifications and log-canonical positivity
proof_stages.summary: A finite-volume complex hyperbolic manifold X_0=H^n_C/Γ is compactified by adding finitely many Baily-Borel cusp points and, after a mild neatness assumption, resolved to a smooth toroidal pair (X,D) with disjoint abelian boundary components and nef big log-canonical divisor K_X+D.

proof_stages.label: Subtlety of canonical positivity
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains why applications require positivity of K_X itself, recalls Mumford’s finite-cover general-type theorem, and uses Hirzebruch surface compactifications to show that K_X need not be nef in dimension 2.

proof_stages.label: Higher-dimensional nef and ample theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is stated: for toroidal compactifications of complex hyperbolic manifolds of dimension at least 3, K_X is nef, and in fact K_X+αD is ample for every 0<α<1.

proof_stages.label: Consequences for cusps and boundedness
proof_stages.summary: Using the ample divisor 2K_X+D, the theorem gives cusp-count bounds in terms of the hyperbolic volume, surface sharpness statements and questions, and effective boundedness/finiteness of toroidal compactifications with bounded volume.

proof_stages.label: Mori-theoretic reduction to extremal contractions
proof_stages.summary: To prove nefness, one assumes a K_X-negative extremal ray, applies the cone and contraction theorems, and uses absence of rational curves in X\D plus nefness of K_X+D to show every contraction fiber has dimension at most 1.

proof_stages.label: Wiśniewski classification of possible contractions
proof_stages.summary: Wiśniewski’s length estimates and classification reduce the possible K_X-negative extremal contractions to either a conic bundle or the blow-up of a smooth codimension-2 subvariety.

proof_stages.label: Bend-and-break exclusion and ample refinement
proof_stages.summary: Hyperbolicity forces each rational fiber to meet the boundary in at least three points; after contracting D to the Baily-Borel compactification, bend-and-break produces impossible reducible fibers in both Wiśniewski cases, completing nefness, while a klt-pair cone/basepoint-free argument gives the ampleness of K_X+αD and surface refinements.

examples.name: Hirzebruch ball-quotient surface compactifications
examples.note: The construction from four elliptic curves on E×E, and its n-torsion variants after blowing up the intersection points, shows K_X can be non-nef in dimension 2, thereby forcing the higher-dimensional formulation and illustrating the sharpness phenomena for cusp bounds.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker constructs the Hirzebruch surface compactifications explicitly from an elliptic curve product, blows up the intersection point(s), checks the boundary curves are disjoint, computes the logarithmic Chern-number equality, and derives consequences for the canonical class.

passes.reason: Hirzebruch’s surface compactifications form a systematic blow-up family, not a sporadic or degenerate object, and are used to refute nefness/positivity of K_X while attaining the sharp surface cusp-count bound.

Hirzebruch's ball-quotient surface compactifications attain the sharp surface cusp-count bound and are used here to show that the canonical class need not be nef, without being trivial, maximally symmetric, or sporadic.

Used to show toroidal ball-quotient surface compactifications need not have nef/ample canonical class, and these highly regular Hirzebruch surfaces are sharp equality cases for the surface cusp bound.

Hirzebruch’s toroidal ball-quotient surface compactifications form an infinite nondegenerate surface family used to show K_X need not be nef, and later noted to attain equality in the sharp surface cusp bound.

Hirzebruch’s toroidal ball-quotient surface compactifications are used to disprove expected nefness/positivity of K_X in dimension 2 and they attain sharp cusp-count bounds, while forming a systematic nondegenerate family rather than sporadic exceptions.

Hirzebruch’s toroidal compactified ball-quotient surfaces are used to show that in dimension two the canonical class need not be nef, and they also attain the sharp cusp-count bound, but they are not degenerate, sporadic exceptional, or maximally symmetric objects.
Used as surface counterexamples to nefness/positivity of K_X, and structurally they are equality cases for the sharp cusp-count bound.

Hirzebruch’s toroidal ball-quotient surface compactifications are used to show that the canonical class need not be nef in dimension two, and they also occur as equality cases for the sharp surface cusp bound.
Used to show that toroidal ball-quotient compactifications can have non-nef canonical class, while this nonsporadic blow-up family also attains the sharp surface cusp-count bound.
Used to show surface toroidal ball-quotient compactifications can have non-nef canonical class, and they also attain the sharp surface cusp-count equality.
Used to show that the compactification canonical class need not be nef, while the systematic Hirzebruch surface family attains the sharp cusp-count bound.
Hirzebruch's toroidal ball-quotient surface compactifications are used to show that the compactification canonical divisor need not be nef, and they also realize the sharp surface cusp-count bound.
